reviews.review_description: I remember spending hours trying to perfect my instant fireball cast when I was little. If this game was still here I think my older self can perfect it now...😢😖
Just wanted whoever controls this IP to know that orbus was fun while it lasted. Thanks for the experience!

I wanted to go back to some of my games the other day and thought "Boy I have not played Orbus for quite a while".  Unfortunately I found out the server had been shut down in April of this year.  I really liked it and wish I had kept playing.  I certainly learned my lesson that these games may not last forever so if you find one you like play it all the way through before it is gone.   Cheers to the developers it was a great game.

I’ve been playing  since I had the quest one and all I have to say is just wow I meant so many friends and it’s honestly gonna be devastating to see the first and in my opinion best VRMMORPG to go goodbye and thank you for all the memories all the kindness in the community and all the fun
Rip orbus

This game had one of the best communities ever. The gameplay was superb, and you could feel the care and passion of the developers in every little thing. It's such a shame that we've gotta lose such a gem of a game, but every second spent on it was more than worth it.

-Superherio

The things that you have done for me to help me get through hard times is endless. I can’t even exsplain the gratitude I owe this game. Orbus VR wasn’t just a game to me it was a lifestyle and nothing can change my mind. Now that it’s gone I’m not sure where to go. You may know me as - StormHammero

Orbus is and always will be the single best RPG game ever made. It truly captures the role playing element of RPGs. The game felt like a second world, my guild members like a second family. This game isn’t the same generic formula of grinding to get higher numbers and better stats so you can press one button. Armor means nothing compared to irl skill. If you gave a brand new player a max level account and a max level and brand new account the old max level would be much more useful. Players can run their own shops, and you’ll almost exclusively be buying from other players. Along with the 8 classes there are “jobs” you can have too and be even more invested in the world. You could fish, make potions, be a harvester, breed dragons, sell dyes, or you could simply just be a mentor and teach new players things you’ve learned. This game captures what SAO was, with an economy, jobs, and classes almost solely determined by the players themselves. The only thing npc’s do is mainly give you quests. All the other vr RPGs like Zenith or Elysium are hollow clones of what this game was and aren’t even worth the time of day.  

To the amazing devs and community, I truly thank you for an incredible 5 years of vr gaming. This game would be nothing without its amazing community and its community would have never met without this game. And to everyone thinking of buying this game I hate to say it but you should rethink it, this game just simply isn’t what it used to be, but I and all the other players will always have the impression this game left on us. And if it’s true that you either die a hero or live long enough to see yourself become the villain, I’m glad Orbus died a hero. Goodbye Orbus Vr. Goodbye to the king of RPGs.

Orbus is officially gone, it was an absolute honor to spend its last moments with its amazing community.

reviews.review_title: A good first attempt at a VR MMO
reviews.review_description:
Updated 2025

RIP Orbus you were fun while you lasted.  Sadly the devs didn't really seem interested in releasing more DLC to keep their financial support going. 

Still worth a play for a bit if you'd like to get a peek into some of the possibilities of a VR MMO, though not without a heavy bit of jank here and there. 

I'd say Ilysia is the only currently viable option to scratch that VR fantasy MMO itch ATM, albeit it is still in Early Access and development.  Though with the last update it definitely feels more like a game instead of just an alpha.  It's come a long way but it's got a long way yet to go, but if you're okay with that it's worth a look. 

Zenith...... Is a reminder of what the devs should never do, I guess it's around but it's more or less dead as far as I know 🤷‍♀️

If you need that MMO itch scratched and a Sci-Fi option peaks ones interest then vendetta online is pretty good too.  So far in my experience a well done VR Port of an older MMO, though it seems to be flying under the radar.  The reviews it has gotten has been overwhelmingly in the positive though 👍

As for Orbus, you got me into VR and had me buying the OG Oculus Quest.  We had fun, I'll always have fond memories of the hundreds of hours I spent with you.  It is time for my musketeer to hang up there gun and put away their turret.  Though they'll always be future adventures to be had in other games.

Updated 2022

Still currently the best VR MMORPG to date IMO.  The newcomer Zenith is a bag of bugs & incomplete mechanics/systems wrapped in a pretty package but not a lot else so far.  The only thing Zenith has over Orbus is maybe subjectively  a "prettier" art style & a more seamless world, less zone loading.

Pros

There’s some good content to be had & there’s been significant improvements since the release.  Though said improvements can & have been slow in coming overall, though I suspect that's more to do with the size of the dev team.  Additional content follows a similar slow release trend & seems to mostly cater to their core end game audience & not to the new/casual player base.  This isn't always the case just seems to happen more often than not, so be aware.

The game includes a fairly decent variety of classes with unique play styles.  Plus you can level and switch between them at will on one character which honestly I feel should be the standard these days IMO.

There is quite an extensive amount of content to be had & is well worth the price of admission.  The player character art style may be a little divisive being highly stylized/cartoony, but if you can get by that I think it looks pretty good overall. The world has also continued to be flushed out visually since the games release.

Cons

I still find myself asking why haven't they added a seated mode in game. Thankfully there is now an accessibility option in the Quest settings out of game..... Still though the dev's never addressed the issue themselves.

The game could still greatly benefit from more robust social features.  Such as more voice chat channels.  A more robust fully featured friends list with maybe either voice messages or at least voice to text instead of the clunky keyboard would be nice when sending messages to offline friends.

I feel like the devs may take for granted that while their PC VR players can use discord and other such social tools their Quest player base cannot easily access the same third party options to make up for the lack of the above mentioned social features.

Some of the classes still feel more complete than others IMO.  Also some of the optimal play styles for a couple of the classes aren't super interesting.  IE Rune mage's who cast a variety of spells & or their higher difficulty versions in an encounter should be more rewarding more than spell spamming speed IMO.  Of course in that example nothing is stopping you from doing just that, but I just have never seen any indication that you won't be lower on the DPS because of it.

A personal gripe....I have played since release & my player journal still has bugged/blank spell page(s) that don't display.....WHY!?  Even after they updated the journal this bug remains for me to this day, for at least one spell.  I hope this doesn't happen to new players anymore, but why has this not been fixed. If this turns out it was fixed.....then why wasn't it retroactively fixed for older characters?

Conclusion

In the end this game still reminds me a lot of early EverQuest honestly.  Back in the day when MMO’s we’re just finding their footing and figuring out what worked & what didn’t etc.  It definitely brought back that feeling I had playing an MMO the first time  

The game has a fairly large player base and the devs are still developing & adding to the game, but some of the core issues still remain. 

Some of the design decisions still feel very dated “early VR”, it’s a tough one to describe but you’ll know it when you see/experience it.

I’d still recommend it for anyone looking for an MMO experience in VR, but my comparison of it feels a lot like EverQuest still remains true.  A valiant first attempt at the genre but may still be overshadowed by future VR MMORPG game releases.

Best 5 years of my life and epic playerbase to be apart of. Super sad it’s getting shut down. Met so many awesome people and friends.

It’s so hard to say goodbye to things in life that have such a strong impact on their lives. I can’t even write how good my experience was over the years because of how much orbus changed my life 

Thank you Orbus -mrches
Was just getting back into and found out it's leaving. Thanks for all the hard work and effort put into this game. I will definitely be playing Eldramoor when yall get it out.
The graphics are terrible, the gameplay is buggy and weak, the music sounds AI generated, and the community has been toxic. I absolutely hate this game. Steer clear of it.

reviews.review_title: Probably the worst MMO of all time
reviews.review_description:
I love mmos but this game is utter trash.
Graphics are abysmal as is the awful combat system and enemy type which there is like only 2.
Was this game made as a bad joke? I’m serious because whoever made it ,,.maybe try another avenue of creative expression. 
Wish I had got a refund , abysmal mmo lol
I tried asking for a refund 20 minutes after playing this garbage game, and have heard absolutely nothing back. Waste of my money and time
Looks horrible. Controls even worse. Regret buying this. Should be free and even then I'd regret downloading this

Unless you want to only play runemage, DO NOT BUY THIS GAME. There is no steady progression for any class besides runemage, and even then that isnt progression, youre OP from the very start! Also, every other player is a runemage, so theres no variety at all. Even playing runemage is almost impossible to get the hang of, but trying to play knight or paladin, I get 2 shot 15 steps away from the city walls! This was a waste of money.

Worse rpg I’ve played, even when you’re 30 feet away enemies will still attack you, you HAVE to be a lvl 20 or up to even think about exploring the map outside the first area highsteep. I mean it’d be a cool game to play except for the fact you have to be a lvl 30 to even do anything, also that’s the cap on leveling?!? Seriously!?
Takes some time to figure out all the mechanics but once you do the game is amazing. Would recommend finding friends to play with as it will help you progress much faster.

I didn’t expect much for the first of its kind, but overly it’s still very poor. The brief introduction leaves most players not knowing what to do and even when you figure some of it out. You can’t find anything.

This game looks like you won it out of a cereal box. With graphics akin to battle for bikini bottom. Not to mention combat is crazy dull. The game gives you absolutely no guidance to where you’re supposed to go or what you’re supposed to do. I’m a huge mmorpg fan and this game feels about as bare bones as it gets. If you do decide to get this game do NOT go wizard. The wand tracking is trash.
This is a really fun game theres so much potential with it if you have 20 Dollars you should buy this

I bought this game not knowing what I was buying. After the first second I booted it up I was hooked. Yet the game wasn’t very with all the controls, many players were more than glad to help. Yes this game does have some bugs and you may crash sometimes, but I don’t let that ruin my experience. I love this game. I totally recommend it.

Before anything I started the game so that I could see how it was before deciding to go past the two hour mark on the refund but so for it was hard to start and was unable to make sense of what the goal was since I didn’t have a dragon and couldn’t get one to complete the race so I want my refund and to get rid of the game but I cannot seem to find how to get my refund
I can’t type in my password because of the lack of an and symbol

Everytime I select a character to log in, it says I lost connection to the server. I have already deleted and redownloaded. I created another character to see if that would help and it didnt. I managed to get a couple of levels in my character when I downloaded the game yesterday, so im not worried if I lose him. But I would like to be able to play.

reviews.review_title: I picked 3 stars because I just started.
reviews.review_description: Idk, I might just might add stars as I learn more stuff because it's alot to do. Real fun though.
With my Oculus Quest, i experience multiple crashes on a daily basis. Tutorial needs more detail. I was completely lost and confused my first time playing.
It’s a good game 100/10 love it just it’s kinda hard to level up...but love ur game just make enemies less harder at least ok love ur game bye
Played a while, fun start but will be better if they can have more support for seared mode. But still worth a buy imo

This is such a fun game. I’ve always enjoyed mmorpg games. Considering that there is one in vr, just can’t beat it. It’s kind of neat how the graphics are cartoon-like. It’s as if it was a cross between Mii’s (for Wii and other Nintendo consoles), World of Warcraft/Runescape, Zelda Windwaker. Very magical game. I feel that if you plan on doing soloing, you’re better off not picking a warrior. However, you can switch between classes (which is great!) I’ve been playing it a lot since I’ve purchased it, and I love it! If your into stuff as listed above, I recommend this game!

The movement is a little janky at first but I got used to it. The combat is nice and having people near by helps out a lot. The community is actually really good compared to some other games. 7/10. Although its good since there's no mmorpgs for quest 2. The graphics are a little lacking but if you like cel-shading then you'll probably like the graphics.
It still has in-game purchase abilities but overall, I love how impressive the world is in VR. If you play it it will take you out of this world and you might need Dramamine.

reviews.review_title: It's a burning ship in the Artics
reviews.review_description:
You rather be on the ship than in the water but the ship is on fire and it's starting to get uncomfortable.

The motor works fine but the captain rather use a sail and now we all get to burn because he's old fashioned.

Orbus almost got to it's destination but slowed down by 70% in the face of adversity. 

They were so proud at the docs seeing us leave and now I know why. 

As our cariage  is charred and in blaze we start to realize, it was preordained.

This was never ment to float, still it tried to show the word it could be a boat.

This Game would be awesome If it would work how it should.
I like the funny Graphik and that every clas is different to Play.
You need about 2hours to learn how to use the Charakters and the Combat System, while that the Game allready crashed 3times.

You need a stable Internet Connection but even with that... Sometimes you can't Talk with npcs, Sometimes your own voice disapear, the hole Game stops, you get Back to Charakter choose menue for no reason... The list ist going on and on.
So it has issues but instead of fixing them, they Bring Out an payed addon.
For me it shows greed and no Heart for the own product.
The second Star is because it had so much Potential but it will be wiped out with the other rpgs coming this year.

I love the uniqueness of this game and the way it utilizes VR to make every class play very differently from the others, but the game doesn't do a very good job of introducing new players to the game.

I'd recommend looking at this video introducing the classes:

https://youtu.be/s6B3wo9I838

And this beginners guide:

https://youtu.be/k3REjz98XVI

They'll benefit you greatly in getting started.

My one complaint is that some of the collision boxes in the game are so big that i had to reach a good 4 feet and snack my hand into walls to open doors.

There are a plethora of spells and combos that you can use and there are quite a few classes as well that you can have fun with. Each class is unique and it’s just a really great game once you get the hang of it. Only thing I can say I don’t like is that not all spells can be recorded in your journal and it crashes. It doesn’t crash often but it does sometimes and it can kind of be a bit nerve wrecking at times. (Could probably just be my wi-fi though). Also, I have a little message for the developers! Please add in a quill and ink set when you pull up your journal so that the players can take their own notes and record the spells that aren’t able to be recorded into the journal! I think it would make the game a whole lot more diverse for every player. That’s all. Love the game guys!

reviews.review_title: My favorite game for the quest
reviews.review_description: BUT the only part i think needs to be clearer is the beginning so for the people who are looking at this review looking for games this game if you like wizard 101, or any rpg game with a good community youll like this so getting to the beginning (dont worry theres nothing lore related) talk to randell and when you talk to the two people the arrows wont go back to randell but you have to go back that simple it takes awhile cause everyone just follows the arrows without reading the journal

This is my first VR game and my first review was very harsh. I’m still getting over the VR sickness that comes with first owning a system.

At first I tried bard because I am a professional musician IRL. This is not a great first class to pick! 

So I tried Runemage...VERY HARD.

Then I tried ranger and THIS IS SO FUN.

This is a game I will want to try all the classes on.

Edit: back down to 2 stars. People are pretty much dickheads unless you suck their ego d. i. c. k. s. Or they are 8 years old....

I am level 7 and I pretty much am fighting the same sh.I.t. I did at level 1...

Worth $20 and that’s it

Edit: so are we just gunna ignore the staying in combat bug? After restarting the app for the third time after getting this bug with three consecutive mobs...I think I’m done with this game. It REALLY pulls you out. FIX THE FOOKIN 
BUG

This is the first and only available vr mmorpg right now, which makes it a must buy product.
Unfortunately this is all about pros.

Cons:
- The graphics is not good but still suitable.
- Not newbies friendly.
- Strange mechanics. Like every class has his own mini game to perform during the battle. Some of it even looks interesting, but exists in a stupid way. Like a mage here has to memorize and draw runes in the air which is challenging since it needs to be done without any mistake. It would be fun if after all the hard drawing you cast something really powerful that insta kills a mob or does a lot of area damage. But instead you do just regular dps and have to spam a "B" sign like an idiot. Which in the end of the day just makes you pick a Scoundrel (a class with a handgun) and do no brain spamming of shots.
- Lack of diversity. As I said there is no regular classes here since the mechanics are pretty strange and annoying. The problem could be solved by adding a system of talents or specs. So you would have this rune mage, but also some classic fire or ice mage and can choose your own play style. Still there is no such system in a game.

The conclusion:
This is the first game of it's kind and would be a must buy and must play as long as there are no competitors nearby. But I'm afraid the first alternative that comes to the market (for example Ilysia) will kill it. Cause people want classic wow like game style and user friendly experience.

As for me, I enjoy it since there are no alternatives.
This game is very epic
